programme.title: Kitchen Nightmares Censored
programme.desc: Chef Ramsay returns to a few restaurants to see if they took his advice. The eateries he visits include Mojito, The Junction (formerly Flamango's) and Bazzini.

programme.title: Kitchen Nightmares Censored

programme.title: Kitchen Nightmares Censored

programme.desc: Chef Ramsay gives the contestants a chicken challenge that includes catching live fowl. The winners spend a day riding in go-karts while the losers prepare chickens for the next service. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen Censored
programme.desc: The remaining chefs get a demonstration in dessert preparation using sound waves and liquid nitrogen, then must steam, poach or boil protein-based dishes. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen Censored
programme.desc: The contestants prepare Hawaiian dishes for a high school's twentieth reunion. The winners spend the day on a yacht while the losers transform Hell's Kitchen to look Hawaiian. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen Censored
programme.desc: The chefs prepare five dishes that must include at least one type of beer. The winners attend the Long Beach Grand Prix IndyCar race while the losers unload kegs of beer and bags of ice. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen Censored
programme.desc: The contestants need to be at their sweetest when Chef Ramsay gives them a dessert challenge. The winning team flies to Sin City while the losers prep the kitchen for Hell's Kitchen's "Date Night". Contains strong language.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen Censored
programme.desc: The remaining chefs are put to a blind taste test. The winners ride horses in the Hollywood Hills while the losers peel grapes. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen Censored
programme.desc: The seven remaining competitors visit New York City, where they tour BLT Steak. When they return to Hell's Kitchen, their challenge is to prepare ethnic dishes. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen Censored
programme.desc: The teams must prepare three dishes. The catch? One must be made in thirty minutes, one in twenty and the last in only ten minutes. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen Censored
programme.desc: In this episode's challenge, each chef must create something special from Alaskan salmon. The winner gets a helicopter ride and lunch at LA's Water Grill. The losers prep fish for the dinner service. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen Censored
programme.desc: The final four chefs eat a dish prepared by Chef Ramsay. For their challenge, they must recreate it in 35 minutes. The winner goes to a baseball game while the losers move furniture out of the dorms. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen Censored
programme.desc: The final four chefs eat a dish prepared by Chef Ramsay. For their challenge, they must recreate it in 35 minutes. The winner goes to a baseball game while the losers move furniture out of the dorms. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen Censored
programme.desc: While wearing new white dinner jackets, the final contestants prepare a menu consisting of four appetizers, four entrees and three desserts. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen Censored

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen Censored
programme.desc: The two teams must cook a meal for sixty newly naturalized U.S. citizens. The winners go on a safari trip in San Diego while the losers do manual labor at the Ballona Wetlands.

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: Iron Chef Chen engages Challenger Lian Su Qin using konnyaku, a unique Japanese healthy food.

programme.title: IronChef

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: Japan's culinary media is dominated by today's challenger, a food critic, musician and graphic artist.

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: The Iron Chef Italian is challenged by a chef who never ceases to pack his restaurant with customers.

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: Morimoto fights a traditionalist chef who fills his dishes with nostalgia.

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: Meiji Kinenkan is the oldest bridal hall in Japan undergoing an upgrade to celebrate its 50th anniversary. This includes the addition of a Chinese restaurant and its new grand chef.

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: Iron Chef Morimoto battles with Challenger Tsuyoshi Kajimoto as they innovate with abalone, a shellfish.

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: The Iron Chef is called upon to face Miyoko Sakai, a famed Thai food critic who has had the privilege to learn the secrets of royal Thai cuisine straight from the source.

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: Iron Chef Morimoto faces another traditionalist who is infamous for his temper, his steadfast discipline and his adherence to tradition.

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: The challenger in this battle of peach is Masanobu Watabe, owner of Le Rameau d'Or in Yokohama, Japan. Watabe runs the entire 22-seat restaurant by himself.

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: Sakai battles the "conjurer of garlic," a chef who embraces the pungent ingredient as real Italians do. Sakai is further challenged to use live squids in the dishes.

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: Three years after losing to Iron Chef Chen, the Italian restaurant Sabatini demands a rematch, this time sending in the master chef of their Tokyo restaurant.

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: Led by Tadamichi Ohta, the chef who defeated Sakai, the Ohta faction invades Kitchen Stadium to uphold traditional Japanese cuisine. Fuji is team Ohta's first contender against Morimoto.

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: In honor of the Girls' Festival, Kaga invites a challenger from the ancient city of Kamakura, Motohito Kondo, who was trained in Kyoto and is an expert in both tea and sake.

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: The American chef Ron Seigel challenges Iron Chef Sakai in this battle of lobster. Who will emerge victorious?

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: The first female challenger at Kitchen Stadium, the 26-year-old Kagata defeated Chef Chen. Years later, she returns to fight Chen again with milk-fed veal.

programme.title: Suitcase Killer Murder Trial
programme.desc: The State's only rebuttal witness wraps up his testimony; the jury will deliberate.

programme.title: Suitcase Killer Murder Trial
programme.desc: Closing arguments begin with Melanie McGuire's defense team talking to the jury first.

programme.title: Treadmill Abuse Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors learned about how a child could get a lung infection. The Defense claimed that's how Corey Micciolo tragically died in his father's care. The State wanted to push back on that argument claiming instead his father's ongoing physical abuse led to Corey's death. Christopher Gregor's attorney challenged the expertise of the State's pediatrician who happened to specialize in infectious disease. She argued to jurors that six-year-old Corey didn't die of an infection.

programme.title: Treadmill Abuse Murder Trial
programme.desc: The police officer who arrested Christopher Gregor testified in his defense. Nearly a year after Corey Micciolo died, the defendant was arrested and charged for child endangerment and murder. Next, a world-renowned forensic pathologist testified for the defense. Dr. Michael Baden, known for his work in famous autopsies like JFK and Martin Luther King, Jr., was consulted by Christopher Gregor's attorneys. The aim was to bring to light how Corey may not have been killed.

programme.title: Treadmill Abuse Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors got a lecture from the defense's forensic pathology expert. After reviewing case notes and the autopsy report, Dr. Michael Baden concluded Corey Micciolo most likely died of an infection and not blunt-force trauma, as the State asserted. Dr. Baden's expertise in this case was three-fold. One, forensic pathology. Two, child abuse. And three, blunt-force trauma.

programme.title: Treadmill Abuse Murder Trial
programme.desc: A second medical opinion to how six-year-old Corey Micciolo was relayed to the jury. On the stand was the Defense's forensic pathologist, Dr. Michael Baden, who happened to specialize in child abuse and blunt force trauma. None of which he claimed defendant, Christopher Gregor, did to Corey to lead to his death. The process and results of conducting an autopsy was made clear to jurors.

programme.desc: The murder of Carol Sue Klaber is cold for decades until new technology can finally bring her killer to light.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: Scottsdale Spree Shooting follows a fast-paced investigation into a killing spree that claimed six victims.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1985, the body of 23-year-old Tonya McKinley is found lying on a street in Pensacola, Florida. Detectives can't find the killer. Decades later, a new forensic science technique finally reveals who murdered Tonya that fateful New Year's Eve.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1976, 16-year-old Pamela Maurer leaves a friend's house to walk to a nearby location for a soft drink. She never makes it home. The next morning, road workers discover her body along College Road. New DNA technology solves the case in 2019.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Teresa Solecki is murdered in 1984 in California. After more than 30 years, DNA technology reveals the killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.desc: On February 26, 1993, an explosion rocked the World Trade Center parking garage and took six people's lives.

programme.desc: After a young woman went missing, police suspected that her well-connected, married ex-lover was responsible.

programme.desc: A teenager was shot to death on a hunting ground in Virginia. His death was initially considered an accident but investigators soon suspected foul play. Years later, a man contacted the FBI with a complex story of conspiracy and murder-for-hire.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On a cold afternoon in 1989, Marine Captain Shirley Russell failed to report for duty at the base in Quantico, Virginia. FBI agents noticed her husband, a former Marine, was acting strangely. There was no body, no witness and no weapon.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.desc: The bodies of several women began turning up around Vienna, Austria. The FBI and Austrian Federal Police investigated suspected serial killer Jack Unterweger, who became an international outlaw, hopping from country to country to evade capture.

programme.desc: "Pee Wee" grew from a petty criminal into a violent predator. When a teen disappeared, detectives uncovered his escalating brutality, realizing he had far more blood on his hands than anyone suspected.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: An investigation into the man known as the 'Babes in the Wood Killer,' who evaded justice for thirty years.

programme.desc: A father took his children out for food after murdering his wife, then strangled them when they returned home.

programme.desc: In March 2005, the gardaí recovered human body parts from the Royal Canal in Dublin. The victim's head was missing. He was identified as Farah Swaleh Noor, a Kenyan with a history of violence against women.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In October 2004, Rachel O'Reilly left her home to drop her children off at school. Less than 20 minutes after returning home she was murdered. Her husband, Joe O'Reilly, staged the crime scene to make it look like a burglary gone wrong.

programme.desc: Musician and special needs teacher Jane Longhurst vanished during her day off work after making a plan to go swimming with her friend's boyfriend. Five weeks later, Jane's partially decomposed body was found burning 25 miles from Jane's home.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: William appeared to be a friendly gardener, but his battered van hid a horrifying secret. It served as a mobile prison where he killed women before dumping their bodies in an isolated area he chillingly called his "garden."

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: After cold-blooded killer Leo Boatman murdered two campers, he went on the kill three inmates behind bars.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Remorseless murderer Casey Pigge's main ambition is to be the ultimate serial killer. If unrestrained, he remains a deadly threat.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Convicted of shooting a man and 21 other felonies, Miti Maugaotega Jr. receives one of the longest prison sentences in Hawaiʻi history. Behind bars, he joins a prison gang and becomes a tornado of death and destruction.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: One of the most feared men in US prison history, Thomas Silverstein became a gang leader for the Aryan Brotherhood on the inside. After savagely killing multiple inmates and a correctional officer, he spent 36 years in solitary confinement.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Feared L.A. crime boss Ezequiel Romo ruthlessly runs the streets of L.A. - even from behind bars. Leading the Blythe Street gang from his cell, he kills with a single phone call, coldly ordering the murders of anyone who stands in his way.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of 27-year-old Pawel Relowicz, who raped and killed 21-year-old Hull student Libby Squire in a case that made headlines around the UK.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Dayton Leroy Rogers, known as The Molalla Forest Killer, mutilated and killed at least eight women in the US.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of one of the most prolific serial killers in Canadian history, dubbed The Pig Farmer Killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A suspect's former lover comes forward with a tale of murder, mutilation and cremation, but there's no way to test the story's validity until a plant pathologist and a dendrochronologist conduct tests where the cremation supposedly occurred.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police investigate a missing college girl and discover her double life: attending school by day and working as a call-girl by night. Her body is found and investigators find her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the mid-1980s, bodies of nude women were found on a remote hill of California. The police got a break when a known prostitute escaped an attacker and was able to identify him and his automobile.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police hoped the shoe impressions found at a crime scene would put their investigation back on track.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An 82-year-old woman was found dead. Clues on the victim's body would tell police what happened that night.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a wealthy real estate tycoon goes missing, it appears to be foul play. In his will, he leaves instructions in case he should die under violent circumstances - which were carried out after a hiker comes across his bullet-ridden skull.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother of two vanishes after a shopping trip. Her body is discovered a month later. Witnesses say they saw the victim being forced into a car by an unknown person.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A killer tried to incinerate and destroy everything that could link him to his crime. But in doing so, he inadvertently created new forensic evidence, evidence which came to light with a technique never before used in a criminal investigation.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A millionaire and his family were executed in their own home. For three years, the murders went unsolved. Finally, a box of ammunition and some fluorescent fibers revealed the ultimate betrayal.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The killer probably hoped to cover his tracks by staging the crime scene. But investigators saw through the attempt almost immediately.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Even though their daughter had run away before, she'd always come back. Her parents were sure this time would be no different, but they were wrong.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An aspiring model turned up dead and the prime suspect was her boyfriend. When he was eventually cleared, investigators had to dig deeper to find the perpetrator.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Avis Banks and her unborn child were brutally murdered. The body was discovered by her fiancé, Keyon Pittman. When police learned that Pittman was having affairs with other women, he became the prime suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a young woman disappeared, police feared she was the latest victim in a string of similar crimes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Doctors don't know why a scientist is gravely ill. When tests reveal the cause, it's too late to save him.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial rapist is on the loose in a Texas town. No one knew where he'd strike next, but the audacity of his attacks and the escalation of violence against his victims made finding him imperative.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1988, when a patient dies unexpectedly in the office of California neurologist Richard Boggs, police begin what they think will be a routine investigation. Soon they uncover a bizarre story of corpse stealing, fake identity and sexual perversion.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is shot to death in her store just one day before she is to testify against the man accused of robbing her. The robber becomes the prime suspect, but he has a solid alibi.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1986, Kathleen Lipscomb's body was found on a deserted road outside of San Antonio. Months passed, then years, and the crime was never solved.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1995, when off-duty Maine State Trooper Vicky Gardner is attacked by Steven Fortin during a routine stop, it triggers a chain of events which jumpstarts a stalled murder case in New Jersey.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bruce Miller was shot to death in the office of a junkyard business. When a computer forensics expert examined the computers owned by the victim's wife, he found all the evidence needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man riding a bicycle was fatally injured, and police believed he was the victim of a hit-and-run accident. Tiny clues found at the scene created a picture of the vehicle which struck him and led police to its driver.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, Patrick McRae was found dead in his Des Moines home and the crime scene was awash with blood.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When 19-year-old Lori Auker left her Pennsylvania home for work and never arrived, it took space-age technology, cat hairs and insects to pinpoint the image of the woman's abductor.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Nancy Newman and her two daughters are found brutally murdered. The crime scene has no shortage of evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, 17-month-old Josh Hinson died in a fire which a federal agency ruled was intentionally set by Josh's mother Terri Strickland. Using the then-emerging resources of the Internet, Terri Strickland undertook her own investigation.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, Marie Robards suffered the loss of her father due to a heart attack - but it was more sinister.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the body of a young woman is found under an isolated highway ramp, a unique fiber on the victim's clothing leads San Diego police straight to the most unlikely of killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Alaskan hikers discover a body wrapped in sheets which were edged in orange stitching. Investigators find a tuft of red carpet fibers clinging to the sheet - threads of evidence which lead them straight to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Shortly after daybreak in Vancouver, British Columbia, a fire was set in a dumpster. No one saw either the arsonist or the fire, and it burned for hours in the deserted parking lot.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a day of fishing in a small, quiet village in Switzerland, a teenage boy did not return home as planned. The investigation revealed some important microscopic evidence in the water near where he was last seen.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother of two vanishes after a shopping trip. Her body is discovered a month later. Witnesses say they saw the victim being forced into a car by an unknown person.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappeared along a California highway in 1991. Three years later, the case was solved when a man's refrigerated truck was looked upon with suspicion.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Soon after becoming pregnant by Dr. Maynard Muntzing, firefighter Michelle Baker's health quickly deteriorates. Eventually, she raises suspicions that Muntzing is doing her harm.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial rapist is on the loose in a Texas town. No one knew where he'd strike next, but the audacity of his attacks and the escalation of violence against his victims made finding him imperative.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three seemingly unrelated deaths proved to be serial murders. The killer used poison which had no taste.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Dianna Green was brutally attacked in her own home and her unborn child was killed. After coming out of a coma and regaining her memory, she is able to identify her husband as the perpetrator.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A couple is caught having sex in a place where no one is supposed to be. A bag of highly explosive material is found attached to a mailbox. A drone spots a large marijuana growing site. A man puts his fist through a motel window.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A sting operation results in the recovery of over 12 ounces of meth. An alarm goes off at a coffee shop. A report of a stolen vehicle at a gas station.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County

programme.sub-title: Four Punks with Guns Making Nightmares That Are Real

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police visit a known drug house in Lake Tahoe. A convicted felon has a weapon. A burglar tries to escape in a stolen vehicle.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Just Can't Save Them All from Themselves
programme.desc: Police and rescue workers respond to a vehicle that collided with a tree. A car full of teens is pulled over. The investigation of a suspicious vehicle in a freeway parking area leads to the arrest of a gang member.

programme.desc: A deputy responds to a fight at a popular bar. A person is screaming in a mall parking lot. Two people are caught breaking into an apartment.

programme.desc: A deputy responds to a house fire several days before Christmas. Later, he plays Santa to some kids who lost everything in the fire.
